title: RISC-V Studio
description: A complete RV32IMAFCV + Zicsr assembler, cycle-stepping emulator, and time-travel debugger in the browser — with the compressed (C) extension, a three-ring privilege architecture (M/S/U) and a real hardware Sv32 MMU (two-level page-table walker, V/R/W/X/U/G/A/D bits, TLB, page faults, trap delegation). NEWEST: the RISC-V VECTOR extension (V / RVV 1.0 subset) — length-agnostic SIMD with a 32-register vector file (VLEN=128, ELEN=32, element widths e8/e16/e32), the vsetvli/vsetivli/vsetvl dynamic vtype+vl configuration model and LMUL register grouping (1,2,4,8 and fractional 1/2,1/4,1/8). Implemented end to end: vector loads/stores (unit-stride vle/vse, strided vlse/vsse, INDEXED gather/scatter vluxei/vsuxei and packed-mask vlm/vsm — all through the MMU), the integer arithmetic core (vadd/vsub/vrsub, vand/vor/vxor, vsll/vsrl/vsra, vmin/vmax, vmul/vmulh*, vdiv/vrem) in vector-vector/vector-scalar/immediate forms, fused multiply-accumulate (vmacc/vnmsac/vmadd/vnmsub), mask-producing compares (vmseq..vmsgt) plus the full mask algebra (vmand/vmor/.. .mm, vcpop.m, vfirst.m, vid.v, viota.m, vmsbf/vmsif/vmsof.m), reductions (vredsum/vredmax/..), permutes (vslideup/down, vslide1up/down, vrgather) and the scalar-element moves (vmv.x.s/vmv.s.x, vmv.v.*, vmerge) — every op the genuine RVV encoding in the OP-V opcode, woven through the assembler, decoder, disassembler, interpreter, BOTH microarchitecture timing models, the syntax highlighter, a live vector-register inspector (decoded vtype + per-register SEW lanes), a strip-mined SAXPY+reduction example, the docs, AND the time-travel journal (stepping backward through a vector op is exact). NEWEST: the full RISC-V BIT-MANIPULATION extension (Zba + Zbb + Zbc + Zbs) — shift-and-add address generation (sh1add/sh2add/sh3add), count-leading/trailing-zeros + popcount (clz/ctz/cpop), signed & unsigned min/max, rotates (rol/ror/rori), sign/zero extension, byte OR-combine + byte-reverse (orc.b/rev8), CARRY-LESS MULTIPLY (clmul/clmulh/clmulr — the kernel of CRCs and GF(2) arithmetic) and single-bit set/clear/invert/extract (Zbs). Every op is the ratified Zb* encoding woven into the OP/OP-IMM opcode space, so the assembler, decoder, disassembler, the executor AND the pipeline + out-of-order timing model all understand it with no special-casing; shipped with a guided Zb example program and 40+ new self-tests covering both execution results and full encode→decode→disassemble→re-assemble round-trips. NEWEST: a microarchitecture PERFORMANCE LAB. A trace-driven timing model layers a classic 5-stage in-order pipeline (IF/ID/EX/MEM/WB) over the unchanged interpreter's retired-instruction stream — so functional results stay byte-for-byte identical while you see how fast real hardware would run the program. It models data hazards with optional data FORWARDING (and the unavoidable one-cycle load-use bubble), no-forwarding write-before-read stalls, multi-cycle EX latencies (mul/div, fp add/mul/div), configurable BRANCH PREDICTORS (static, 1-bit, 2-bit bimodal, gshare) over a BTB with a side-by-side accuracy comparison, and a set-associative I-cache + D-cache hierarchy (size/block/associativity/LRU-FIFO/write-back-through). It reports total cycles, CPI/IPC, a where-the-cycles-go stall breakdown, cache hit/miss tables, and the textbook instruction × cycle PIPELINE DIAGRAM with stalls and mispredictions flagged — every knob re-running the model live, and the cycle counts proven against hand-computed oracles in the in-app suite. NEWEST: a from-scratch OUT-OF-ORDER SUPERSCALAR core — a real Tomasulo dynamically-scheduled machine reading the same retired trace beside the in-order model: REGISTER RENAMING (so WAR/WAW false dependences vanish), a REORDER BUFFER for precise in-order commit, unified RESERVATION STATIONS with out-of-order wakeup/select onto a configurable pool of typed pipelined/iterative functional units, a bandwidth-limited common data bus, and a LOAD/STORE QUEUE with address disambiguation and store-to-load forwarding. It extracts instruction-level parallelism (IPC climbs past 1 toward the issue width on independent work, stays ≈1 on a true dependence chain), hides long-latency divides behind independent work until the ROB is too small to see past them, and charges a DYNAMIC misprediction penalty that grows when a branch resolves late — reported as the speed-up over in-order, ROB occupancy, functional-unit utilization, a dispatch-bottleneck breakdown (ROB/RS/LSQ-full, front-end-starved), and a colour-coded instruction-lifetime (Gantt) diagram, all proven against hand-derived schedules and whole-program structural invariants in the in-app suite. All built on the existing machine-mode trap & interrupt architecture (mtvec/mepc/mcause, mret, a CLINT timer), a from-scratch C compiler that lowers a real subset of C to RISC-V, IEEE-754 float, atomics, CSR counters, a memory-mapped framebuffer, syscall console, disassembler, shareable links, and a 110+ check in-app verification suite — all strictly additive, so with paging off the machine is byte-for-byte the bare-metal RV32 it always was.
